description_long: This object is a tall, upright stone monument resembling a stylized sword or knife embedded in a rectangular stone base, surrounded by patches of green moss or grass. The blade is thick and rectangular, with a weathered, rough surface and visible chisel marks, giving it an ancient, monumental appearance. The front face features deep vertical grooves and a slightly pointed tip, while the sides are relatively flat and unadorned. The rear face is plain, with subtle textural variations and no visible engravings or ornamentation. The base is irregular and appears to be made of the same stone as the blade, blending into the surrounding ground cover. The overall form suggests a symbolic or decorative purpose, possibly as a public art installation or commemorative marker in an urban park or plaza.
description: A tall, weathered stone monument shaped like a stylized sword or knife, set in a stone base with patches of green moss. The blade features deep grooves and a rough, ancient texture.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow edge of the stone blade, with visible weathering and some vertical markings.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the broad face of the blade with deep vertical grooves and a slightly pointed tip, set in a mossy stone base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, showing the plain, flat edge of the blade with minimal markings and a continuation of the mossy base.
description_view_3: Rear view highlighting the back face of the blade, which is mostly plain with subtle grooves and a rough surface.
attribute_handle_design: no distinct handle, blade extends directly into the base
